SILVER IMPORT CURBS MAY TRIGGER SUPPLY SHORTAGE ; HARSHAL BAROT 

• 🪙 India could face a silver shortage during the upcoming festive and wedding season if import restrictions continue

• 🚢 Silver imports under most HS codes require DGFT licences, limiting fresh supplies

• 📦 Domestic demand is currently being met through existing inventories, recycled silver and ETF selling

• 📈 Tight supply has pushed silver premiums to around 10%, with fresh imports remaining very low

🟢 Impact: Positive for domestic silver recyclers and organised jewellers with strong inventory; negative for silver-dependent manufacturers and traders due to supply constraints.

SONATA SOFTWARE: PROVIDES UPDATE ON US LEGAL PROCEEDINGS ⚖️

• 🇺🇸 Sonata Software North America (SSNA) had filed an involuntary Chapter 7 bankruptcy petition against OBSA Operating Company LLC

• ❌ The US Court dismissed the petition as a sanction against OBSA due to procedural non-compliance

• 📑 OBSA has instead executed an Assignment for the Benefit of Creditors (ABC) under California law as an alternative to Chapter 7 bankruptcy

• 💰 SSNA is listed among the out-of-court creditors under the ABC process

🟡 Impact: Neutral (Primarily a legal process update, with recovery of dues now expected through the ABC mechanism instead of bankruptcy proceedings.)

reports.text:
🚨 BUFFETT INDICATOR HITS RECORD HIGH AS US STOCK VALUATIONS STRETCH 📈⚠️

• 📊 Buffett Indicator (US Market Cap-to-GDP) has climbed to a record ~230%, versus a long-term average of 89%

• 💻 The Dot-Com Bubble peaked at around 140%, well below current levels

• 📈 Most major S&P 500 valuation metrics are at or near record highs, with the P/E ratio being a notable exception

• 💰 Rising leverage, strong AI optimism and persistent inflows continue to support elevated valuations

• ⚠️ A richly valued market may become more vulnerable if earnings growth disappoints or interest rates remain higher for longer

🟡 Impact: Cautious/Neutral (The indicator signals expensive valuations but is not, by itself, a timing tool. Elevated valuations can persist for extended periods if earnings and liquidity remain strong.)

👟 Liberty Accelerates Growth With 100 New Stores

Liberty is entering a new growth phase with plans to open 100 stores, including exclusive Healers outlets, while investing ₹75 crore in retail expansion, brand building, and customer experience.

📌 Key Highlights

🏬 Opening 100 new stores, expanding the retail network beyond 600 outlets.

💰 Investing ₹75 crore: ₹50 crore for retail expansion and ₹25 crore for marketing & brand building.

🎯 Streamlined the portfolio from 10 sub-brands to 6 focused brands.

👟 Healers contributes nearly 40% of revenue, while Leap 7X contributes around 20%. Both are expected to become standalone brands in the next 3–4 years.

🛍️ Exclusive Healers stores to open across airports, malls, and pop-up formats.

✨ Rolling out modern store formats to enhance customer experience.

🤖 Investing in AI-driven engagement.

🏭 Manufacturing investments focused on automation and efficiency, not capacity expansion.

🌍 Exports contribute less than 5% of revenue, with significant long-term potential.

OVOBEL FOODS: CRISIL DOWNGRADES CREDIT RATING TO 'BB/STABLE' ⚠️📉

• ⭐ CRISIL Ratings revised the company's long-term bank loan rating to CRISIL BB/Stable from CRISIL BB+/Stable, while reaffirming the short-term rating at CRISIL A4+ under the 'Issuer Not Cooperating' category

• 🏦 The revised rating applies to ₹29.90 crore of bank loan facilities, including cash credit and term loans

• ⚠️ CRISIL cited non-availability of adequate information from the management for continuous monitoring, leading to the 'Issuer Not Cooperating' classification

• 📝 Ovobel Foods said it is initiating the withdrawal process and believes the rating agency did not adequately consider its current financial performance, which could impact borrowing costs

• 📢 The company said it will keep the stock exchanges informed of any further material developments regarding the rating matter 

🔴 Impact: Negative (The credit rating downgrade and 'Issuer Not Cooperating' classification could increase borrowing costs and weigh on lender and investor confidence.)

TATA POWER: COMMISSIONS 100.8 MW JEWALI WIND PROJECT IN MAHARASHTRA 🌬️⚡

• 🌬️ Tata Power Renewable Energy Ltd. (TPREL) has commissioned its 100.8 MW Jewali Wind Project in Dharashiv, Maharashtra

• ⚡ The project is expected to generate 299 million units of clean electricity annually and offset over 245 million kg of CO₂ emissions every year

• 🏙️ The power generated will be supplied to Tata Power Mumbai Distribution, supporting its Renewable Purchase Obligation (RPO) compliance

• 📈 With this commissioning, TPREL's total renewable utility capacity has reached 11.6 GW, including 6.7 GW operational and 4.9 GW under implementation

• 🎯 The project strengthens Tata Power's renewable portfolio and supports its goal of achieving 100% clean energy generation by 2045 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The commissioning expands Tata Power's renewable capacity, enhances clean energy generation and strengthens long-term growth visibility.)

reports.text:
Powerica
Recently, there is lot of hype around anything related to data centers...

This is a newly listed stock, and there was certainly lot of hoopla around it as 15% of the orderbook is from data centers and fast growing.

Did some deep dive - 
It seems the company is more of a systems integrator + EPC business for gensets. It gets critical parts like engine, alternator etc. from Cummins and builds the gensets along with installation.

Also, all the major genset orders from hyperscalers go directly to Cummins. Only the small-scale desi data center or edge data center orders come to Powerica

Basically, its a low-tech low-margin business...nothing to get excited about.

The other business is wind power - IPP + EPC, which is having its own challenges.

- The government's two-year exemption from Public Procurement Order (PPO) restrictions allows four Chinese electrical equipment manufacturers to participate in PSU tenders for high-voltage transformers and GIS switchgear until domestic manufacturing capacity expands. reports.text:
Global Semiconductor Supply Chain Remains Highly Concentrated

Key Highlights:

🏭 TSMC controls over 80% of the world's leading-edge (<16nm) chip production, powering most AI servers and premium smartphones.

🌏 Taiwan, South Korea, and China account for ~85% of global semiconductor foundry revenue, highlighting regional concentration.

💾 Samsung and SK Hynix produce ~70% of the world's DRAM memory chips.

⚙️ ASML holds a near-100% monopoly in EUV lithography, the critical technology required to manufacture chips below 7nm.

🇺🇸 US companies generate around 60% of global chip design revenue, while China accounts for ~38% of global OSAT (chip packaging and testing) capacity.

🏗️ 56% of global wafer capacity still lies in mature nodes (≥34nm), creating an opportunity for emerging semiconductor manufacturing hubs like India.

SPML INFRA: ICRA UPGRADES CREDIT RATING TO BBB (STABLE) ⭐📈

• ⭐ ICRA upgraded SPML Infra's long-term credit rating to [ICRA]BBB (Stable) from [ICRA]BBB- (Stable)

• 🏦 ₹380 crore term loan and ₹200 crore cash credit facilities were upgraded to [ICRA]BBB (Stable)

• 💼 ₹1,000 crore non-fund based facilities were upgraded to [ICRA]BBB (Stable)/[ICRA]A3+, with the rating also assigned for the enhanced amount

• 📊 ₹15 crore proposed fund-based facilities were also upgraded to [ICRA]BBB (Stable)

• 💰 The rating actions cover total bank facilities of ₹1,595 crore, reflecting an improved credit profile 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The credit rating upgrade enhances SPML Infra's borrowing profile, improves lender confidence and supports future funding.)

JPM on Bajaj Fin

OW, TP raised to Rs 1215

1Q’FY27 update underscored

(i) strong growth momentum with AUM rising by an impressive 7.2% q/q (vs JPMe at 6.6%) to Rs 5.47tn. AUM growth accelerated to 23.9% y/y vs. 22.4% in 4Q’FY26 and came in ahead of JPMe of 23.1%

reports.text:
The Zee promoter family is on a continuous aim to increase their shareholding in Zee Entertainment by utilizing offshore Mauritius entities.

Here is a quick breakdown of how this has been playing out:

 *​What is Sunbright?* 
It is a Mauritius-based investment vehicle owned by the Subhash Chandra/Goenka promoter family. It used to be called Essel Holdings but was rebranded to Sunbright to distance it from past debt issues.

 *​The Sony Merger Connection:* 
When the Zee-Sony merger was being planned, Sunbright was the exact entity set up to receive shares and non-compete fees from Sony. The goal was to help the family infuse money back into the merged company to keep their ownership stake. That fell through when the merger was cancelled.

​ *Past Shareholder Rejection & FCCBs:* 
They actually tried a similar fundraise via Sunbright last year to pump 2,237 crore Rupees into Zee. However, institutional shareholders banded together and voted it down because they disapproved of the pricing. Following that rejection and the withdrawal of the fundraise, Zee had to look elsewhere for cash and ended up issuing 239 million dollars in Foreign Currency Convertible Bonds (FCCBs) to raise immediate funds.

Follow the Beat The Street | Stock Market Insights channel on WhatsApp: https://whatsapp.com/channel/0029Vb8R9fH47XeEgsFs7T2s

 *​What is happening now:* 
Now they are pushing again. The Zee Board just approved a fresh, massive plan to issue 24.95 crore warrants to Sunbright worth over 3,143 crore Rupees at 126 Rupees per share. If it clears, the promoter family's ownership stake will jump from just 4% to around 20-24%.

​Basically, they are using the same offshore entity from the failed Sony deal to try and regain control of Zee. It still faces the major hurdle of getting shareholder approval, which tripped them up last time.

🚀 *JTL Defence (BSE: 537254)* 📊

*From NCLT Distress to a Precision Defence Turnaround*

🔹 *The Big Pivot:* Formerly a distressed listed entity (RCI Industries), it was acquired by steel tube major *JTL Industries* via the NCLT route. Rebranded officially to *JTL Defence* on April 1, 2026. Promoters now hold a tight 95% stake.

🔹 *Manufacturing Asset:* Fully operational plant in *Baddi, Himachal Pradesh* specializing in high-precision processing of copper, brass, stainless steel, and specialty alloys for defense/OEM requirements.

Follow the Beat The Street | Stock Market Insights channel on WhatsApp: https://whatsapp.com/channel/0029Vb8R9fH47XeEgsFs7T2s

🔹 *Smart Business Model:* Moving rapidly into *Job Work"*(accounts for ~30% of volumes). Clients supply raw materials, allowing JTL to extract high processing margins while staying completely shielded from raw material price volatility.
🔹 *Financial Turnaround (FY26):*
 * *Revenue:* Jumped to ₹21.02 Cr (vs just ₹98 Lakhs in FY25).
 * *Profitability:* Swung to a positive net profit (PAT) with an impressive 21.8% EBITDA margin for the full year.
 * *Balance Sheet:* Cleaned up post-NCLT; D/E ratio sits at a comfortable 30.5%

🔹 *₹100 Crore Fundraise:* Shareholders just approved a *₹100 Crore QIP/Preferential Issue* on June 30, 2026. Funds are earmarked for upgrading the plant for higher-margin defense empanelments and will naturally dilute promoter holding to invite institutional investors.

⚠️ Watchout: Stock has high weekly volatility and has run up strongly (hitting a fresh 52-week high of ₹506.8 on July 2, 2026), pricing in a lot of forward growth.

reports.text:
GNG Electronics Strengthens India's Refurbished ICT Play with Redington Partnership

Redington is India's largest IT products distributor with a deep network across Tier 1/2/3 cities, enterprise customers, education institutions and resellers.

Instead of building this network organically over years, GNG gets immediate access to one of India's strongest ICT distribution ecosystems.

India's refurbished laptop market remains largely fragmented and unorganized.

By partnering with a trusted distributor like Redington:
=> Refurbished products become easier for enterprises to procure.
=> Electronics Bazaar gets institutional credibility.
=> Corporate buyers gain confidence due to warranty-backed products.

The company is not solely relying on direct sakes or online marketplaces, but they are strengthening distributor network, reseller ecosystem and system integrators.

The partnership enhances GNG Electronics' distribution capabilities, strengthens its position in the organized refurbished ICT market, and can accelerate domestic growth without significant incremental investment in sales infrastructure

reports.text:
The Supreme Court has issued a stern warning on the use of AI in courts.

In a landmark ruling, it struck down an NCLT order after finding reliance on fake and AI-generated legal precedents, comparing such hallucinated judgments to a "methyl isocyanate leak" in the justice system. The court has adopted a zero-tolerance stance and stressed that AI can assist, but human judgment must remain in control.

PC Jeweller's turnaround continues to gather pace.

The company reported a ~21% YoY growth in consolidated revenue for Q1 FY27.

But the bigger highlight is on the balance sheet.

 - Outstanding debt has been reduced by 90%+ since the September 2024 settlement with banks.
 - Another 24% reduction was achieved during Q1 FY27.
 - Management expects the company to become completely debt-free in the current quarter.

From debt restructuring to nearly debt-free in less than two years—it's been a remarkable turnaround.

Now the key question is whether the company can sustain this operational momentum after cleaning up its balance sheet.

Bernstein on Trent

O-P, TP cut to Rs 3500 from Rs 5000

While Zudio has overshadowed Westside in store adds over last 5+ years, Westside is still 40% of Trent’s total retail area & surprised on store

additions in F26 after lackluster F23-F25 years

Proprietary store data indicates 3 things for Westside

(1) Store rationalization is over and helped Trent move away from certain cities & markets,

(2) Stores additions indicate focus in Top-100 cities only with city-densification underway

(3) Pin-densification is not visible (unlike what Trent did with Zudio)

Believe approx +50 stores/year is an achievable target for next 4-5 years, which will take them to ~500-550 stores.

For context, Pantaloons has 400 may add 20/year.

Hence, do not see whitespace as an issue and with a clearer strategy, expect them to achieve this target.

CLSA on NHPC

High Conviction O-P, TP Rs 117

NDR takeaways,

Key message from NHPC was FY26 saw start of two of its largest projects, driving regulated equity (RegEq) up 25% YoY, but a delay in Parbati 2 (P2) tariff & restoration of Teesta deflated PAT by c.20%.

Management addressed clients’ biggest concerns about PAT growth by clarifying its conservative accounting policy has deflated FY26 PAT, but once it secures final tariff for P2 HEP, full PAT will be recognised retrospectively with interest, so there is no loss of value on an NPV basis.

NHPC is biggest beneficiary of Indus water treaty being put in abeyance and it has awarded two of the four hydro projects already, which are sub four-year projects

Forecast REE (ex-minority) growth of 90% over FY26-30CL versus 31% growth over past five years

With a step-change in REE, forecast its EPS to double & its ROE to expand 750bps over FY26-28CL.

Atharva Polyplast looks more than just a plastic component maker.
What stands out is its end-to-end manufacturing setup — from design and tooling to moulding, finishing, and assembly — all under one roof.

A few things make the story interesting:

Strong compliance stack helps it qualify for global customers
Margins have improved sharply, driven by a better product mix
Engineering services can become a meaningful third growth engine
The company has scale-up headroom, backed by land, capacity, and premium market opportunity

In simple words, Atharva seems to be positioning itself as a precision manufacturing partner for global players, not just a commodity supplier.

If execution stays on track, this could be a business worth tracking closely.

**Prestige Estates – Morgan Stanley View**

Morgan Stanley remains bullish on Prestige Estates, citing strong pre-sales momentum, resilient demand, growing leasing income and attractive embedded value in its rental portfolio.

**Brokerage View**
- Rating: Overweight
- Target price: ₹1,920
- Positive long-term outlook
- Strong execution continues

**Key Takeaways**
- Strong start to 1QFY27
- Pre-sales: ₹6,300 crore in first two months
- Achieved ~18% of FY27 estimate
- Ahead of expected run-rate

**Demand Outlook**
- Demand remains healthy
- Stable footfalls and conversions
- ASPs remain resilient
- No meaningful AI-related demand impact in Bengaluru or Hyderabad

**Leasing Update**
- Tower X, BKC: ~70% leased
- Mahalakshmi project: ~10% leased
- Leasing rate around ₹400/sq. ft.
- Commercial portfolio gaining traction

**Hospitality Business**
- Hospitality listing may be deferred
- Awaiting completion of Aerocity hotel
- Aerocity project to have 778 keys
- Listing could unlock value later

**Rental Potential**
- FY29 rental income potential: ₹4,000 crore
- Assumed cap rate: 7.5%
- Rental portfolio worth nearly two-thirds of Enterprise Value
- Highlights undervalued residential and hospitality businesses

**Why It Matters**
- Strong pre-sales momentum
- Healthy commercial leasing
- Significant rental income growth
- Attractive valuation support

**Key Takeaway**
- Morgan Stanley believes **Prestige Estates is well-positioned for sustained growth, supported by strong residential demand, expanding commercial leasing, significant rental income potential and hidden value in its hospitality and residential businesses, while maintaining an Overweight rating with a target price of ₹1,920.**

**Oil Marketing Companies (OMCs) – CLSA View**

CLSA believes the recent sharp fall in crude oil prices provides near-term earnings relief for India's oil marketing companies, but cautions against extrapolating these gains over the long term.

**Sector View**
- Positive near-term outlook
- Lower crude boosts marketing margins
- Earnings recovery expected
- Long-term risks remain

**Key Positives**
- Sharp decline in crude prices
- Retail fuel prices likely to remain unchanged
- Higher auto-fuel marketing margins
- Opportunity to recover past losses

**Why Margins Improve**
- OMCs incurred losses when crude surged
- Stable pump prices allow margin recovery
- Similar situation seen in 2022
- Near-term profitability improves

**Key Risk**
- Government may reverse ₹10/litre excise duty cut
- Fiscal deficit targets could drive policy change
- Margin normalization possible
- Relief may be temporary

**Valuation View**
- Current valuations imply ~₹3/litre marketing margin
- Sustainable only if Brent remains around US$70–75/bbl
- Higher crude could pressure profitability
- Limited upside if oil prices rise

**Preferred Outlook**
- Near-term earnings supportive
- Crude below US$70/bbl is ideal
- Policy decisions remain key
- Monitor fuel taxation closely

**Why It Matters**
- Lower crude boosts profitability
- Excise policy is a major variable
- Margin sustainability uncertain
- Oil prices remain key driver

**Key Takeaway**
- CLSA believes **the recent fall in crude prices offers a welcome earnings recovery for IOC, BPCL and HPCL through stronger fuel marketing margins. However, investors should remain cautious, as any reversal of the ₹10/litre excise duty cut or a rebound in Brent crude above US$70–75/bbl could significantly reduce the sector's long-term upside.**

__Disclaimer: I am not a SEBI-registered Analyst. This post is only for educational and learning purposes. No Buy, Sell or Hold recommendation.__

ABS Marine Services Secures ~ ₹ 126.12 Cr Long-Term Charter Contract for Offshore Support Vessel MV ARTEMIS

ABS Marine Services Limited, one of the leading maritime companies offering comprehensive services in Vessel Ownership, Ship Management, Marine and Port Services has signed a Charter Party Agreement with Hardy Exploration & Production (India) Inc., acting on behalf of itself and its joint venture partners, Oil and Natural Gas Corporation Limited (ONGC) and Invenire Petrodyne Limited, for the deployment of its Anchor Handling Tug Supply (AHTS) Vessel, MV ARTEMIS.

Under the agreement, MV ARTEMIS will be deployed to support petroleum exploration and production activities on the east coast of India. The contract has a firm charter tenure of 1,825 days (5 years) from the date of mobilisation, with an option for extension by up to a further 1,825 days at the charterer's discretion. The contract is valued at approximately ₹126.12 Cr (inclusive of GST) over the firm charter period.

Strategic Impact
The contract reinforces the company’s position as a trusted offshore service provider and enhances revenue visibility through a long-term charter arrangement. The deployment of MV ARTEMIS under a multi-year contract is expected to support stable vessel utilization and strengthen the Company's offshore operations portfolio.

The award also reflects the Company's proven operational capabilities and strong relationships within the offshore energy sector. With increasing focus on domestic exploration and offshore energy development, the company remains well positioned to capitalize on emerging opportunities and further expand its presence in the offshore support services segment.
